description: Benchmark on 36 real de-identified TCGA patient mutation cases (open-access data, cBioPortal)
pipeline: 4-tier: direct FDA match → FDA-approved repurposing → investigational repurposing → custom design

tier_definitions.DIRECT_FDA: Exact or evidence-matched FDA-approved therapy for the variant/context.
tier_definitions.FDA_REPURPOSING: FDA-approved drug for the target/gene but not a direct label match; requires off-label oncology review.
tier_definitions.INVESTIGATIONAL_REPURPOSING: Mechanistically supported non-approved candidate or clinical-stage agent; trial or expanded-access path needed.
tier_definitions.CUSTOM_DESIGN: No suitable known therapy found; medicinal chemistry brief generated for preclinical follow-up.

patients.patient_next_step: No direct or repurposed drug was found for EGFR in Bladder Urothelial Carcinoma. The custom-design brief is a preclinical research handoff for medicinal chemistry teams, not a treatment recommendation for immediate use.

lead_candidates.safety_summary: Risk level: LOW. Flags: hERG:MEDIUM. Safety gate: PASS. All predictions require wet-lab confirmation.
lead_candidates.adme_notes: Multiple ADME issues: BCS Class IV (poor absorption); High hepatic clearance. Significant optimisation required.

lead_candidates.mechanism: Epidermal growth factor receptor erbB1 binding agent
matched_terms: Epidermal growth factor receptor erbB1 binding agent
lead_candidates.safety_summary: Risk level: LOW. Flags: none. Safety gate: PASS. All predictions require wet-lab confirmation.
lead_candidates.adme_notes: No major ADME liabilities identified. Confirm with in-vitro panel.

lead_candidates.safety_summary: Risk level: LOW. Flags: Hepatotox:MEDIUM. Safety gate: PASS. All predictions require wet-lab confirmation.
lead_candidates.adme_notes: Minor ADME liability: BCS Class IV (poor absorption). Addressable by medicinal chemistry optimisation.
